page: Microsoft Azure E8as_v6 is an Eav6-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 with VNNI and BF16 support, AES cryptography, and SHA extensions. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU results placing in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Text processing performance is also in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance, including Geekbench and stress-ng multi-core tests, falls within the average range. Memory performance shows a distinct tradeoff: while cached small-block read bandwidth and large-block write bandwidth are average, uncached large-block read bandwidth is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong, whereas multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and Redis SET operations perform at average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8as_v6 offers a high memory density of 8.0 GB per vCPU, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of local storage. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing power, though the weak uncached memory read bandwidth represents a performance tradeoff for large-dataset operations. This server type is suited for memory-intensive applications, relational database hosting, text processing pipelines, and moderate web serving workloads that benefit from high memory capacity and strong single-core execution.
description: Microsoft Azure E8as_v6 is an Eav6-family virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of memory, utilizing an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance features 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core and supports AVX-512 instructions. It contains no local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance, alongside average multi-core and database execution. However, uncached large-block memory read bandwidth is a noted bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this instance provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads. It is suited for database hosting, text processing, and general-purpose applications requiring substantial memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8as_v6 is an Eav6-family virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, suited for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: An AMD-powered virtual machine featuring high memory density, strong single-core performance, and dedicated compute resources for memory-intensive workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance

Suited for database hosting, text processing, and memory-intensive applications
